SEARCH
Module it.polimi.ingsw.am37
Package it.polimi.ingsw.am37.controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am37.controller.GameController
All Implemented Interfaces:
Observable
public class GameController extends Object implements Observable
The Class GameController's duty is to manage the phases of the game and to give directions to the model, maintaining the view updated.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GameController(Player creator, int numOfPlayers)
    the method GameController sets the game to be started adding the new players and redirecting them to the new lobby.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addPlayer(Player newPlayer)
    the addPlayer method adds a new player to the game before instancing the game, linking to each of them a VirtualView everytime a player is added, proceeds then to call gamePhaseHandler which will set up the players.
    List<Player>
    getAddedPlayers()
    the method getAddedPlayers returns the list of players who got added in the game.
    GameModel
    getGameInstance()
    the getGameInstance method returns the instance of the game created.
    int
    getNumOfPlayers()
    the method getNumOfPlayers returns the number of players participating in the game.
    Map<Player,VirtualView>
    getPlayerViews()
    the method getPlayerViews returns the Virtual View of each player.
    State
    getState()
    the method getState returns the actual state of the game.
    boolean
    isEndGameStarted()
    the isEndGameStarted method returns true if the game is in the final phase, or returns false otherwise.
    boolean
    isGameStarted()
    the isGameStarted method returns true if the game started, or returns false otherwise.
    void
    playerChoosesObjective(Player p, ObjectiveCard c)
    the method playerChooseObjective checks if the player has already chosen his private objectives, if not, it gives the player 2 objectives and makes the player choose one, it updates the player's virtual view then.
    void
    playerChoosesStartCardSide(Player p, StartCard c, Side s)
    the method PlayerChoosesStartCardSide makes the player choose the side of his own starting card, then initiate the Player's Kingdom, calls the gamePhaseHandler to proceed in the player's setup and updates the player's view.
    void
    playerChoosesToken(Player p, Token t)
    the method playerChoosesToken checks if the player already has a token, if not, it makes the player choose a new one.
    void
    playerDrawsCardFromAvailable(Player p, StandardCard c)
    the method playerDrawsCardFromAvailable makes the player draw a card the available ones on the field, proceed then to update the player's virtual view showing the new available ones.
    void
    playerDrawsCardFromDeck(Player p, GoldDeck d)
    the playerDrawsCardFromDeck method makes the player draw a gold card from the deck, then updates the player's virtual view.
    void
    playerDrawsCardFromDeck(Player p, ResourceDeck d)
    the playerDrawsCardFromDeck method makes the player draw a resource card from the deck, then updates the player's virtual view.
    void
    playerPlacesCard(Player p, StandardCard c, Side s, Position pos)
    the method playerPlacesCard makes the player place the card in the position he chose, and with the side he chose.
    void
    setEndGameStarted(boolean endGameStarted)
    the method setEndGameStarted set the attribute endGameStarted to true if the game has entered the final phase.
    void
    setGameInstance(GameModel model)
    the setGameInstance method sets the model as the instance of the game created.
    void
    setGameStarted(boolean gameStarted)
    the method setGameStarted set the attribute isGameStarted to true if the game is started and instanced.
    void
    setState(State state)
    the setState attribute sets the actual state of the game passed by parameter.
    void
    setVirtualView(Player p, VirtualView vv)
    the method setVirtualView sets the Virtual View of the player passed by parameter.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • GameController

      public GameController(Player creator, int numOfPlayers)
      the method GameController sets the game to be started adding the new players and redirecting them to the new lobby.
      Parameters:
      creator - is the player who created the game.
      numOfPlayers - is the number of players participating.

  • Method Details

    • getAddedPlayers

      public List<Player> getAddedPlayers()
      the method getAddedPlayers returns the list of players who got added in the game.
      Returns:
      the participants attribute.

    • getNumOfPlayers

      public int getNumOfPlayers()
      the method getNumOfPlayers returns the number of players participating in the game.
      Returns:
      the numberOfPlayers attribute.

    • getGameInstance

      public GameModel getGameInstance()
      the getGameInstance method returns the instance of the game created.
      Returns:
      gameInstance attribute.

    • setGameInstance

      public void setGameInstance(GameModel model) throws AlreadyAssignedException
      the setGameInstance method sets the model as the instance of the game created.
      Parameters:
      model - is the instance given to the game.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the has already been assigned to a model.

    • getState

      public State getState()
      the method getState returns the actual state of the game.
      Returns:
      the state attribute.

    • setState

      public void setState(State state)
      the setState attribute sets the actual state of the game passed by parameter.
      Parameters:
      state - is the updated state.

    • isGameStarted

      public boolean isGameStarted()
      the isGameStarted method returns true if the game started, or returns false otherwise.
      Returns:
      isGameStarted attribute.

    • setGameStarted

      public void setGameStarted(boolean gameStarted)
      the method setGameStarted set the attribute isGameStarted to true if the game is started and instanced.
      Parameters:
      gameStarted - is the boolean used to pass the information.

    • isEndGameStarted

      public boolean isEndGameStarted()
      the isEndGameStarted method returns true if the game is in the final phase, or returns false otherwise.
      Returns:
      endGameStarted attribute.

    • setEndGameStarted

      public void setEndGameStarted(boolean endGameStarted)
      the method setEndGameStarted set the attribute endGameStarted to true if the game has entered the final phase.
      Parameters:
      endGameStarted - is the boolean used to pass the information.

    • getPlayerViews

      public Map<Player,VirtualView> getPlayerViews()
      the method getPlayerViews returns the Virtual View of each player.
      Returns:
      the PlayerViews attribute.

    • setVirtualView

      public void setVirtualView(Player p, VirtualView vv)
      the method setVirtualView sets the Virtual View of the player passed by parameter.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player who's virtual view is set.
      vv - is the virtual view of the player.

    • addPlayer

      public void addPlayer(Player newPlayer) throws IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, NoCardsException, AlreadyAssignedException
      the addPlayer method adds a new player to the game before instancing the game, linking to each of them a VirtualView everytime a player is added, proceeds then to call gamePhaseHandler which will set up the players.
      Parameters:
      newPlayer - is the player who requested to join the game.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the nickname the player chose was already taken or if the game is full.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the game has already started.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the objectives cards were already given to someone else?

    • playerChoosesStartCardSide

      public void playerChoosesStartCardSide(Player p, StartCard c, Side s) throws IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, NoCardsException, AlreadyAssignedException
      the method PlayerChoosesStartCardSide makes the player choose the side of his own starting card, then initiate the Player's Kingdom, calls the gamePhaseHandler to proceed in the player's setup and updates the player's view.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player who's choosing the starting card.
      c - is the starting card assigned to the player.
      s - is the side of the card the player chose.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the player tries to place another player's starting card.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the players tries to place the starting card in the wrong phase of the game.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the card was already assigned.

    • playerChoosesToken

      public void playerChoosesToken(Player p, Token t) throws AlreadyAssignedException, IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, NoCardsException
      the method playerChoosesToken checks if the player already has a token, if not, it makes the player choose a new one.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player who has to choose the token.
      t - is the token chosen by the player.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the player has already chosen a token, or already has received a hand or the objectives cards.
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the player chooses the black token or an already taken one.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the player tries to choose the token in the wrong phase of the game.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards to create a hand or to give an objective or to set the public objectives in the gameHandler.

    • playerChoosesObjective

      public void playerChoosesObjective(Player p, ObjectiveCard c) throws AlreadyAssignedException, WrongGamePhaseException, IncorrectUserActionException, NoCardsException
      the method playerChooseObjective checks if the player has already chosen his private objectives, if not, it gives the player 2 objectives and makes the player choose one, it updates the player's virtual view then.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player who's choosing the objectives.
      c - is the objective the player chose.
      Throws:
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the player has already chosen an objective.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the player tries to choose the objective in the wrong phase of the game.
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the player tries to choose another player's objective.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.

    • playerPlacesCard

      public void playerPlacesCard(Player p, StandardCard c, Side s, Position pos) throws IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, NoCardsException, AlreadyAssignedException
      the method playerPlacesCard makes the player place the card in the position he chose, and with the side he chose.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player placing the card.
      c - is the card the player's placing.
      s - is the side of the card that the player chose.
      pos - is the position where the player's placing the card.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if it's not the player's turn.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the player tries to place a card in the wrong phase of the game.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the gamePhase handler throws it.

    • playerDrawsCardFromDeck

      public void playerDrawsCardFromDeck(Player p, ResourceDeck d) throws IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, AlreadyAssignedException, NoCardsException
      the playerDrawsCardFromDeck method makes the player draw a resource card from the deck, then updates the player's virtual view.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player drawing the card.
      d - is the card drawn by the player.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the player tries to draw the card when it's not his turn.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the player tries to draw a card in the wrong phase of the game.
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the gamePhase handler throws it.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.

    • playerDrawsCardFromDeck

      public void playerDrawsCardFromDeck(Player p, GoldDeck d) throws IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, AlreadyAssignedException, NoCardsException
      the playerDrawsCardFromDeck method makes the player draw a gold card from the deck, then updates the player's virtual view.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player drawing the card.
      d - is the card drawn by the player.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the player tries to draw the card when it's not his turn.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the player tries to draw a card imn the wrong phase of the game.
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the gamePhase handler throws it.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.

    • playerDrawsCardFromAvailable

      public void playerDrawsCardFromAvailable(Player p, StandardCard c) throws IncorrectUserActionException, WrongGamePhaseException, AlreadyAssignedException, NoCardsException
      the method playerDrawsCardFromAvailable makes the player draw a card the available ones on the field, proceed then to update the player's virtual view showing the new available ones.
      Parameters:
      p - is the player who's drawing the card.
      c - is the card drawn by the player.
      Throws:
      IncorrectUserActionException - if the player tries to draw when it's not his turn.
      WrongGamePhaseException - if the player tries to draw when in the wrong phase of the game.
      AlreadyAssignedException - if the gamePhase handler throws it.
      NoCardsException - if there's no cards left.
